stories.title: drive_detect idle-poll -> active-poll escalation on engine-on detection (B-049)
stories.intent: During Spool's 2026-04-29 inverted-power drill, the engine ran ~1 minute with full alternator-charging signature in BATTERY_V (14.4V at idle, cranking dip 11.4V), BT/OBD adapter connected throughout, but drive_start NEVER FIRED -- zero ECU PIDs captured. Root cause: orchestrator's idle-poll mode only queries BATTERY_V (ELM_VOLTAGE / ATRV adapter-level, ECU-independent). drive_detector waits for ECU-dependent PIDs to start responding, but those PIDs are never queried in idle-poll. Critical: post-B-043 wiring, every key-on = Pi power-on -> Pi cold-boots -> idle-poll -> engine runs whole drive -> drive_start never fires -> ZERO ECU DATA captured. Silent data-loss bug that activates the moment B-043 wiring lands. Fix: when BATTERY_V > 13.8V sustained for N consecutive samples (alternator-active signature), escalate idle-poll to active-poll AND inject single-shot ECU probe of RPM PID. Drive 5 baseline confirms this car's alternator hits 14.4V on bulk-charge, so 13.8V cleanly distinguishes engine-on from any engine-off state.

stories.title: TD-036 close: orchestrator non-blocking BT-connect at lifecycle startup
stories.intent: Per TD-036 (filed Sprint 18 post-deploy): when Pi boots with engine off, orchestrator's _initializeConnection blocks indefinitely in the connect-retry loop because adapter responds but ECU is silent. runLoop never starts -> US-226 auto-sync (in runLoop) never fires. Currently a benign issue (CIO bench-state) but becomes critical post-B-043 wiring: every key-on cold-boots Pi, and if the engine takes >6 connect-retry-cycles to respond on K-line, US-226 auto-sync gets locked out for that drive. Fix: refactor _initializeConnection to be non-blocking after first wall-clock-bounded attempt (e.g., 30s); allow runLoop to start with connection in 'pending' state; runLoop's existing health-check + reconnect path handles late-arriving ECU response. US-226 auto-sync (interval-based) fires on schedule independent of OBD connection state.

stories.completionNotes: Session 116 (Rex / Agent 1) -- US-244 SHIPPED passes:true (Sprint 20 3/10). TD-036 closed. FIX SHAPE: daemon-thread + threading.Event-with-timeout -- _initializeConnection dispatches the connect attempt on a daemon thread named 'initial-obd-connect' and waits via Event with timeoutSec from new config. On timeout the daemon continues running in the background (its eventual success transparently flips connection.isConnected() True; runLoop's existing _checkConnectionStatus tracker observes the late transition and fires _handleConnectionRestored on the next pass). On thread-completion within the cap, success/failure both log + return without raising. On the live (simulate=False) path the connect daemon shares the orchestrator's _shutdownEvent so SIGTERM still wakes its retry-loop sleeps within ~ms (US-232 / TD-035 invariant preserved). NEW METHODS on LifecycleMixin: _initialConnectTimeoutSec() reads pi.obdii.orchestrator.initialConnectTimeoutSec with float-coercion-or-30s-default safety net; _runInitialConnectWithTimeout(timeoutSec) returns (completed, success, error) tuple. CONSTRUCTION-TIME FAILURES STILL FATAL: ImportError of the connection module logs WARN + returns without raising (pre-existing behavior preserved); any other Exception during construction (factory crash, KeyError on bad config) still raises ComponentInitializationError. ONLY retry-exhaustion / wall-clock-timeout is downgraded to WARN+continue. RUNLOOP TOLERANCE: zero behavioral change in core.py runLoop -- the existing tracker (lastConnectionState = self._checkConnectionStatus()) returns False for an unconnected connection and fires no transition until isConnected() flips True. stories.title: B-047 US-C: Pi update-checker daemon -- poll server registry, decide if update needed
stories.intent: Pi-side daemon that periodically (configurable interval, default 1 hour) polls the server's GET /api/v1/release/current endpoint, compares the server's version to the Pi's local deploy/RELEASE_VERSION, and decides whether an update is needed. If update available, writes a marker file (e.g., /var/lib/eclipse-obd/update-pending.json) with target-version + downloaded-from URL + rationale; the actual apply step (US-248) reads this marker. Daemon honors safety preconditions: do NOT check for updates while a drive is in progress (drive_id != None) -- drive-state is sacred. Runs as part of orchestrator runLoop or as a separate systemd timer (Marcus + Rex pick during grooming).

stories.completionNotes: Session 118 (Rex / Agent 1) -- US-247 SHIPPED passes:true (Sprint 20 5/10). B-047 US-C Pi update-checker daemon LIVE. SHAPE: UpdateChecker class (src/pi/update/update_checker.py, NEW ~330 lines incl headers/docstrings) with constructor DI for httpOpener (default urllib.request.urlopen) and isDrivingFn (zero-arg callable). Reads pi.update.* for policy (enabled, intervalMinutes, markerFilePath, localVersionPath); reuses pi.companionService.baseUrl + apiKeyEnv for transport (single source of truth -- the Pi has one server it talks to). check_for_updates() returns a typed CheckResult dataclass(slots=True) carrying {outcome, localVersion, serverVersion, markerPath, rationale}. CheckOutcome StrEnum has 8 terminal states: UPDATE_AVAILABLE / UP_TO_DATE / SKIPPED_DRIVING / DISABLED / LOCAL_VERSION_UNAVAILABLE / SERVER_NO_RECORD / NETWORK_ERROR / CONFIG_ERROR. The method NEVER raises -- every error path resolves to a typed outcome so the runLoop caller can be exception-free. DECISION ORDER: drive-state gate (highest priority -- sacred) -> enabled gate -> API-key gate -> local .deploy-version readable gate -> server fetch -> server-payload validateRelease() check -> parseVersion() comparison. Only UPDATE_AVAILABLE writes the marker file. SERVER 503 (US-246 contract for 'no record stamped') maps to SERVER_NO_RECORD (clean skip, no marker, no error log) -- Pi treats it as 'no update available' per acceptance contract. SERVER 5xx / network exception -> NETWORK_ERROR (transient; next interval retries). MARKER FILE: {target_version, server_url, rationale, checked_at} JSON written atomically with parent-dir mkdir; existing marker overwritten so the latest decision wins (US-D apply step is the only consumer). ORCHESTRATOR WIRING: lifecycle._initializeUpdateChecker (gated on pi.update.enabled, soft-fail on construction error) hands the checker an isDrivingFn closure that resolves _driveDetector lazily (defensive late-resolution -- detector reference can be re-bound by lifecycle paths) and returns False when detector absent (open-by-default safety: a missing detector must NEVER block all update checks). core._maybeTriggerUpdateCheck (parallel shape to US-226's _maybeTriggerIntervalSync) gates on _updateCheckIntervalSeconds elapsed since _lastUpdateCheckTime, calls check_for_updates, exception-isolated so a runtime crash is logged + swallowed. runLoop calls it once per pass right after _maybeTriggerIntervalSync. CONFIG: 4 new DEFAULTS (pi.update.enabled=true, pi.update.intervalMinutes=60, pi.update.markerFilePath=/var/lib/eclipse-obd/update-pending.json, pi.update.localVersionPath=.deploy-version). config.json mirrors. stories.completionNotes: Session 119 (Rex / Agent 1) -- US-248 SHIPPED passes:true (Sprint 20 6/10). B-047 US-D Pi auto-update apply step LIVE. SHAPE: UpdateApplier class (src/pi/update/update_applier.py NEW ~470 lines) with constructor DI for subprocessRun (default subprocess.run) + 3 fail-OPEN safety closures (isDrivingFn, getPowerSourceFn, getLastObdActivitySecondsAgoFn). Reads pi.update.* for policy (applyEnabled, stagingPath, rollbackEnabled, markerFilePath, localVersionPath). apply() returns a typed ApplyResult dataclass(slots=True) carrying {outcome, targetVersion, priorRef, rationale, rollbackOutcome}. ApplyOutcome StrEnum has 13 terminal states: SUCCESS / NO_MARKER / MARKER_INVALID / DISABLED / SKIPPED_DRIVING / SKIPPED_BATTERY_POWER / SKIPPED_RECENT_OBD_ACTIVITY / DRY_RUN_FAILED / DEPLOY_FAILED / POST_DEPLOY_VERIFY_FAILED / ROLLBACK_OK / ROLLBACK_FAILED / CONFIG_ERROR. The method NEVER raises -- every error path resolves to a typed outcome so the runLoop caller has no try/except for expected failure modes (defensive Exception catch at outer boundary as last-resort). DECISION ORDER (load-bearing ordering): (1) marker presence + shape (NO_MARKER / MARKER_INVALID -- malformed marker auto-cleared), (2) safety gates: drive-state (sacred -- highest priority) -> power source (BATTERY blocks to avoid dirty mid-shutdown) -> recent OBD activity (engine just turned on but detector hasn't fired yet) -- gates check BEFORE the applyEnabled gate so the operator log shows 'would have been safe to apply' instead of hiding under the disabled flag, (3) applyEnabled gate (CIO opt-in safety net -- defaults to FALSE so production never applies until CIO flips the flag), (4) capture priorRef via 'git rev-parse HEAD' (failure -> CONFIG_ERROR, no state mutation), (5) git fetch -> git checkout targetVersion -> deploy-pi.sh --dry-run -> deploy-pi.sh full -> verify post-deploy .deploy-version.version == targetVersion. ANY phase failure invokes _maybeRollback(priorRef) which (when rollbackEnabled=true) runs 'git checkout priorRef' + 'sudo systemctl restart eclipse-obd'; ALWAYS clears marker (success or rollback or rollback-skipped) so a poisoned target version cannot re-trigger on the next interval tick (atomicity invariant). FAIL-OPEN INVARIANT: missing or raising safety closures default to 'safe to apply' (isDrivingFn None -> not driving; getPowerSourceFn None -> external; getLastObdActivitySecondsAgoFn None -> no recent activity). Rationale: applyEnabled=false is the load-bearing safety net; per-gate fail-open ensures a broken UpsMonitor / detector / DB cannot perma-block updates indefinitely. The 5-minute (300s) recent-OBD-activity threshold is hardcoded (not config) -- it's load-bearing for the safety contract; a story follow-up can lift it to config if vehicle-by-vehicle tuning is needed. ORCHESTRATOR WIRING: lifecycle._initializeUpdateApplier (gated on pi.update.enabled, soft-fail on construction error; mirrors _initializeUpdateChecker shape) constructs the UpdateApplier with 3 lazy closures: (a) isDrivingFn resolves _driveDetector at call time (matches US-247 pattern); (b) getPowerSourceFn resolves HardwareManager.upsMonitor at call time, returns 'external' on missing component; (c) getLastObdActivitySecondsAgoFn runs SELECT (julianday('now')-julianday(MAX(timestamp)))*86400 FROM connection_log against the live DB, returns None on no rows / no DB / SQL error. core._maybeTriggerUpdateApply (parallel shape to _maybeTriggerUpdateCheck) gates first on a CHEAP fast-path probe (UpdateApplier.markerExists() = single Path.is_file() call) before doing anything else -- when no marker exists the runLoop pass adds essentially zero overhead. When marker IS present, gates on _updateCheckIntervalSeconds elapsed since _lastUpdateApplyTime (cadence symmetry with check), invokes apply, exception-isolated. runLoop calls it once per pass right after _maybeTriggerUpdateCheck. CONFIG: 3 new DEFAULTS (pi.update.applyEnabled=False [CIO opt-in], pi.update.stagingPath=/tmp/eclipse-obd-staging, pi.update.rollbackEnabled=True). config.json mirrors. SCOPE FENCE NOTES: (1) Story scope.filesToTouch listed `tests/pi/update/test_update_applier.py` only; created two test files (the unit tests for the applier itself + an orchestrator integration test for the runLoop wiring) -- the integration test is structurally parallel to Session 118's test_orchestrator_update_check.py and pins the closure-wiring fail-open invariants. (2) Story acceptance text says 'skip if connection_log shows recent OBD activity within last 5 min' -- implemented as a SQL query in the orchestrator-side closure, threshold hardcoded to 300s in the applier (not pulled to config because the threshold is load-bearing for the safety contract; promote to config in a follow-up only if vehicle tuning emerges as a need). (3) Story scope.filesToTouch listed `deploy/deploy-pi.sh (UPDATE -- ensure --dry-run flag exists and exits 0/non-zero appropriately)` -- the --dry-run flag already exists at deploy-pi.sh:117 from prior sprint work; verified by inspection; no changes needed. (4) Story scope mentioned `git stash`-style apply but the implementation uses simpler `git checkout priorRef` for rollback -- equivalent semantics for a single-branch deploy, simpler to test, and matches the stagingPath model (a stash would only be needed if we were carrying uncommitted local changes which the deploy model doesn't). VERIFICATION: new tests 39/39 pass (25 UpdateApplier unit tests + 14 orchestrator integration tests) in 45.67s; orchestrator+obdii+sync+update regression 614 passed/1 skipped/0 regressions in 126.08s; full fast suite 3660 passed/17 skipped/1 FAILED (922.34s) -- the one failure is the same pre-existing TD-040 V0.18.0 seed test broken by main@5025508 Sprint 19 close PM version bump (unrelated; same failure all of Sessions 114/115/116/117/118/119); ruff clean on all touched files (after auto-fix of unused imports in tests); validate_config.py OK; sprint_lint 0 errors / 27 pre-existing warnings (US-248 introduced none). 3660 = 3621 (Session 118 baseline) + 39 (new tests) -- math checks out. stories.completionNotes: TD-039 closed via two-layer remediation (b) + (c) per the TD's own remediation plan. (b) offices/pm/prds/_template.md created NEW (file did not exist -- 5th sprint in a row hitting story-scope drift to a phantom file; created the canonical PRD template in flight, leading with the 'Schema Impact' gate as a 3-checkbox question: server migration required yes/no/N/A). (c) scripts/schema_diff.py implemented: loads Pi SQLite schema by executing the canonical CREATE TABLE constants under src/pi/* in an in-memory sqlite3 connection (column extraction delegated to PRAGMA table_info -- no fragile regex), loads server schema by walking SQLAlchemy Base.metadata.tables, emits a deterministic JSON diff with sort-stable ordering. Two design decisions worth preserving: (1) gate trips ONLY on the TD-039 silent-data-loss direction (Pi has columns server lacks); server-side extras (analytics columns, PK strategy diffs) are reported in sharedTableDrift for visibility but do NOT fail the gate, because they don't risk data loss and would otherwise drown CI in by-design noise. (2) Two allowlists keep the gate signal-only: SERVER_MIRROR_COLUMNS (source_id/source_device/synced_at/sync_batch_id -- US-CMP-003 sync convention) and PI_PK_RENAMED_TO_ID (battery_health_log.drain_event_id and calibration_sessions.session_id -- documented in producing modules' Sync shape blocks). Smoke run on current schemas: 17 Pi tables, 19 server tables, 11 shared, 3 reporting drift (drive_summary/profiles/vehicle_info -- all server-side-only by-design extras), 0 gate failures, exit 0. Tests 23/23 pass in 47s (15 pure-function tests for computeDiff covering empty/single/PK-rename/mirror/table-presence/column-drift/sort-determinism/summary-counts; 4 loader smoke tests pinning real-schema integration; 4 CLI exit-code tests). SCOPE FENCE NOTES: (1) Story scope.filesToTouch listed 'offices/pm/prds/_template.md (UPDATE)' but file did not exist -- created NEW with canonical template shape including the 'Schema Impact' gate. (2) Story scope.filesToRead listed 'src/pi/data/database_schema.py' but Pi schema lives at src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py + 6 sibling schema modules (drive_summary, dtc_log_schema, pi_state, sync_log, calibration.types, power.battery_health); loaded all seven into the canonical Pi-schema registry.
